 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.2.3","title":"Add post-session wrap-up checklist (artifacts + memory outcomes)","description":"# Goal
Ensure every cockpit session ends with a consistent wrap-up:
- publish compiled artifact into the thread
- (optional) persist artifact to repo (explicit command)
- (optional) record `cm outcome` and apply

# Acceptance
- Runbook includes a checkbox checklist for session end.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:06:36.014522628-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:16:17.160197748-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:16:17.160197748-05:00","close_reason":"Added post-session wrap-up checklist incl optional cm steps to specs/cockpit_runbook_v0.1.md","labels":["cockpit","docs","memory"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.2.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:06:36.015799712-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.2.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:06:36.01883699-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.3","title":"ntm project integration (optional .ntm prompt palette + templates)","description":"# Goal
Decide whether this repo should ship a minimal `.ntm/` directory so `ntm` can:
- discover Brenner Loop prompt templates
- provide a command palette for common session actions
- reduce copy/paste friction

---

# Why this matters
If we treat `ntm` as the cockpit primitive, project-local templates are the ergonomic win.

---

# Constraints
- Keep file sprawl minimal.
- Prefer a tiny set of templates that reference existing prompt docs / artifact schema.

---

# Acceptance
- Clear decision: commit `.ntm/` (and what’s inside) OR keep ntm config external.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:05:48.839677537-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T21:05:48.839677537-05:00","labels":["cockpit","ntm","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:05:48.840819437-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:05:48.843859469-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.3.1","title":"Determine minimal .ntm template surface for Brenner Loop","description":"# Goal
Identify the smallest useful set of ntm project templates/commands.

Use ntm docs as source-of-truth:
- how `.ntm/` is discovered
- how templates are organized
- how the command palette loads prompts

# Acceptance
- A short list of proposed templates (names + purpose + where they live).
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:05:57.42669944-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T22:11:35.14800316-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T22:11:35.14800316-05:00","close_reason":"Documented minimal .ntm template + palette surface proposal in specs/cockpit_runbook_v0.1.md","labels":["design","ntm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.3.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:05:57.456708679-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.3.2","title":"Implement minimal .ntm templates + palette commands","description":"# Goal
If we decide to commit `.ntm/` to this repo, add a minimal set of project templates.

Suggested templates:
- kickoff (Round 0)
- request structured delta reply
- request adversarial critique / third alternative
- request discriminative tests table

Keep it small: this should be “the 20% that gives 80% of the cockpit ergonomics”.

# Acceptance
- Running `ntm palette` in a session shows Brenner-specific commands.
- Templates reference the canonical artifact schema / delta spec.
","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:06:08.027469314-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T13:49:26.147666782-05:00","labels":["cockpit","docs","ntm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.3.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:06:08.047829459-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.3.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:06:08.053667308-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.3.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.1.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:06:08.055359834-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.3.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.1.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:06:08.057183337-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.3.2","title":"Implement minimal .ntm templates + palette commands","description":"# Goal
If we decide to commit `.ntm/` to this repo, add a minimal set of project templates.

Suggested templates:
- kickoff (Round 0)
- request structured delta reply
- request adversarial critique / third alternative
- request discriminative tests table

Keep it small: this should be “the 20% that gives 80% of the cockpit ergonomics”.

# Acceptance
- Running `ntm palette` in a session shows Brenner-specific commands.
- Templates reference the canonical artifact schema / delta spec.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:06:08.027469314-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T13:52:38.217919627-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T13:52:38.217919627-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented .ntm/templates (check_mail, reply_delta, discriminative_tests, adversarial_critique) and .ntm/config.toml palette","labels":["cockpit","docs","ntm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.3.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:06:08.047829459-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.3.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:06:08.053667308-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.3.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.1.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:06:08.055359834-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.3.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.1.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:06:08.057183337-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.3.3","title":"If we don’t commit .ntm/: document external ntm setup","description":"# Goal
If we choose not to commit `.ntm/` to this repo, still make ntm usage easy by documenting:
- recommended `ntm spawn` command
- recommended pane counts (1 per agent type)
- how to use `ntm send` with prompts generated by `brenner`

# Acceptance
- A user can follow the doc and get the cockpit running in < 5 minutes.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5sq","title":"Bug: Edge proxy should gate Cloudflare Access header trust","description":"Security consistency: apps/web/src/proxy.ts currently trusts Cloudflare Access headers unconditionally (hasCloudflareAccessHeaders(request) || hasValidLabSecret(request)). This conflicts with apps/web/src/lib/auth.ts which only trusts CF Access headers when BRENNER_TRUST_CF_ACCESS_HEADERS=1.

If proxy.ts is used as middleware (it exports config.matcher), the unconditional trust re-opens header spoofing risk.

Plan:
- Align proxy.ts with auth.ts by gating CF Access trust behind BRENNER_TRUST_CF_ACCESS_HEADERS=1.
- Keep BRENNER_LAB_SECRET path working regardless.

Acceptance:
- BRENNER_LAB_MODE=1 + CF headers present + BRENNER_TRUST_CF_ACCESS_HEADERS unset + no valid lab secret => deny (404).
- BRENNER_LAB_MODE=1 + CF headers present + BRENNER_TRUST_CF_ACCESS_HEADERS=1 => allow.
- BRENNER_LAB_MODE=1 + valid lab secret => allow (even if trust env unset).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"PurpleCat","created_at":"2025-12-30T05:40:23.596596275-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T06:11:16.181191661-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T06:11:16.181191661-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed proxy.ts to gate CF Access header trust behind BRENNER_TRUST_CF_ACCESS_HEADERS=1 and added unit tests.","labels":["security","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5sq","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-2wh","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T05:40:23.602098622-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5xh","title":"Fix unified kickoff memory section placement","description":"# Problem\n\nWhen running `./brenner.ts session start --unified --with-memory`, the MEMORY CONTEXT block was appended into the "TRANSCRIPT EXCERPT(S)" section instead of being its own section.\n\n# Fix\n\n- Add `memoryContext` support to prompt composition and render it after the excerpt.\n- In unified session kickoff, pass memory context separately (do not concatenate into excerpt).\n\n# Done when\n\n- Unified kickoff prompt contains "## MEMORY CONTEXT (cass-memory)" as a top-level section (not nested inside the excerpt).\n","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-30T05:25:27.168202295-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T05:31:02.213565142-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T05:31:02.213565142-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed unified session start memory injection: composePrompt accepts memoryContext and unified kickoffs pass it separately (not appended to TRANSCRIPT EXCERPT(S)).","labels":["cli","memory"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5xh","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.6.2.2","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T05:25:27.193542098-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
